title: Nexo Review 2026: Earn Up to 14% APY on Your Crypto (Honest Take)
excerpt: If you're sitting on Bitcoin, ETH, or stablecoins and doing nothing with them, Nexo lets you earn passive yield or borrow against your holdings without selling. Here's everything you need to know before you sign up.
author: CHAEI PUEI Tech

affiliateCta.label: Try Nexo Free →
affiliateCta.description: Earn up to 14% APY on crypto & stablecoins. No lock-up. Withdraw anytime.

content:
## Why I Started Looking at Nexo

As a developer, I accumulate crypto the same way most people in tech do — through projects, side income, and holding positions I don't want to sell. The problem: crypto sitting in a cold wallet earns zero.

I started looking at Nexo after reading about it on a few finance forums. The promise: earn interest on your crypto, or borrow against it without selling and triggering a taxable event.

After using it for several months, here's my honest take.

> **📢 Affiliate disclosure:** This article contains an affiliate link to Nexo. If you sign up through my link, I may earn a commission at no cost to you. I only recommend products I've personally used.

## What Is Nexo?

Nexo is a centralized crypto financial platform founded in 2018 and based in Zug, Switzerland. It offers three core products:

1. **Nexo Earn** — Deposit crypto or stablecoins and earn interest automatically
2. **Nexo Credit Line** — Borrow cash or stablecoins against your crypto holdings without selling
3. **Nexo Exchange** — Buy, sell, and swap 60+ cryptocurrencies

As of 2026, Nexo holds over $7 billion in assets and serves 7+ million users across 200+ countries.

## Getting Started — Account Setup

The onboarding process is straightforward:

```
1. Sign up at nexo.com (email + password)
2. Verify identity (KYC — passport or national ID)
3. Enable 2FA (strongly recommended)
4. Deposit your first asset
```

KYC is mandatory — expect a photo ID scan and a selfie. Verification typically takes 5–30 minutes. This is standard for any regulated financial platform.

> **💡** Complete your KYC immediately after sign-up, before making any deposits. Some features are restricted until verification is complete.

## Feature 1: Nexo Earn — Passive Yield on Your Crypto

This is the main reason most people join Nexo.

**How it works:** You deposit assets into your Nexo Earn wallet. Interest accrues daily and compounds. You can withdraw at any time — there's no lock-up by default.

### Current Earn Rates (indicative — rates change)

| Asset | Base Rate | Rate w/ NEXO tokens |
|---|---|---|
| USDC / USDT | up to 12% APY | up to 14% APY |
| BTC | up to 5% APY | up to 7% APY |
| ETH | up to 5.5% APY | up to 7.5% APY |
| NEXO | up to 16% APY | — |
| EUR / USD | up to 8% APY | up to 10% APY |

**Two payout options:**
- **In-kind** (earn BTC if you deposit BTC) — standard rate
- **In NEXO tokens** — bonus +2% APY on top of the base rate

### Loyalty Tiers

Nexo has a 4-tier loyalty system based on what % of your total portfolio is in NEXO tokens:

| Tier | NEXO % | Earn Bonus | Loan Rate |
|---|---|---|---|
| Base | 0–1% | — | 13.9% |
| Silver | 1–5% | +0.25% | 11.9% |
| Gold | 5–10% | +1% | 8.9% |
| Platinum | 10%+ | +2% | 5.9% |

For most users, staying at Base or Silver tier is fine. Going Platinum makes sense only if you're a heavy Nexo user.

## Feature 2: Nexo Credit Line — Borrow Without Selling

This is where Nexo gets interesting for long-term crypto holders.

**The problem it solves:** You hold 1 BTC worth $80,000. You need $20,000 for a car. You don't want to sell your BTC because:
- You'd pay capital gains tax
- You'd lose your position if BTC pumps next month

**The Nexo solution:** Deposit your BTC as collateral, borrow $20,000 in USDC or fiat. Keep your BTC position. Repay whenever you want — no fixed schedule.

```
Example:
- Collateral: 1 BTC ($80,000)
- Max loan-to-value (LTV): 50%
- Max borrow: $40,000
- Borrow $20,000 USDC at 6.9% APY (Platinum tier)
- Monthly interest: ~$115/month
```

**Key loan mechanics:**
- No credit check, no income verification
- No fixed repayment schedule
- Interest accrues daily, auto-deducted from account
- Minimum loan: $50
- Supported collateral: BTC, ETH, BNB, XRP, and 40+ assets

### Loan-to-Value Ratios

Different assets have different LTV ratios:

| Collateral | Max LTV | Maintenance LTV | Liquidation LTV |
|---|---|---|---|
| BTC | 50% | 70% | 83% |
| ETH | 50% | 70% | 83% |
| Stablecoins | 90% | 95% | 100% |

> **⚠️** If your collateral drops in value and LTV breaches the maintenance threshold, Nexo will issue a margin call. If it hits the liquidation threshold, your collateral gets automatically sold. Monitor your LTV ratio closely in volatile markets.

**Liquidation example:**
```
You borrow $20,000 against 1 BTC at $80,000 (25% LTV)
BTC drops to $24,000 → LTV is now 83% → liquidation triggered
Nexo sells your BTC to cover the loan
```

The safest approach: keep your LTV below 30% to give yourself a large buffer during downturns.

## Feature 3: Nexo Exchange

Nexo has a built-in exchange for 60+ assets. It routes orders through multiple liquidity providers to get competitive rates.

**Honest assessment:** The exchange is convenient for swapping assets already in your Nexo account, but the rates aren't always the best compared to Binance or Coinbase for large trades. Use it for convenience — not for active trading.

**NEXO Token Cashback:** There's up to 0.5% cashback on exchange volume paid in NEXO tokens if you're Gold/Platinum tier.

## Security — What You Need to Know

This is the most important section. Nexo is a centralized custodial platform, meaning they hold your assets. Before depositing significant funds, understand the risks:

**What Nexo does right:**
- Custodial insurance: $375 million coverage via Lloyd's of London and others
- Proof of reserves: on-chain audited
- SOC 2 Type 2 certified
- 2FA, biometric auth, withdrawal address allowlisting
- Licensed and regulated in multiple jurisdictions

**Risks to be aware of:**
- Centralized custodian — you don't control your private keys
- Regulatory risk: crypto regulations are evolving in every country
- Platform risk: like any fintech, there's operational risk

**My personal approach:** I use Nexo for stablecoin yield (USDC/USDT) and a portion of my ETH. I keep the majority of my BTC in cold storage. Never deposit what you can't afford to have locked up temporarily in a worst-case scenario.

## Nexo vs. Competitors

| | Nexo | Binance Earn | Coinbase Earn |
|---|---|---|---|
| Stablecoin APY | up to 14% | up to 10% | up to 5.1% |
| BTC Earn | up to 7% | up to 5% | ❌ |
| Crypto-backed loans | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| No lock-up option | ✅ | Flexible plans only | N/A |
| Fiat withdrawal | ✅ | Limited by region | ✅ |
| Available regions | 200+ | 100+ (US limited) | US + select |

For pure stablecoin yield with flexibility, Nexo consistently offers higher rates than most alternatives.

## Pros and Cons

**What I like:**
- Daily interest accrual — you can see earnings every single day
- No lock-up on most assets — full liquidity if you need it
- The loan product is genuinely useful for avoiding taxable events
- Clean mobile app, clear UI
- Earn paid in NEXO token gives notably higher rates

**What I don't like:**
- KYC is mandatory for everything
- Exchange rates aren't always competitive for large swaps
- NEXO loyalty tier creates a two-tier system — Base tier rates are lower
- Limited support for some DeFi-native assets
- Customer support response time can be slow during high-volume periods

## Who Should Use Nexo?

**Good fit:**
- Long-term crypto holders who want yield on idle assets
- People who want crypto-backed liquidity without selling (and triggering taxes)
- Stablecoin holders wanting yield above bank savings rates
- Developers with crypto income looking for a simple earn product

**Not the right fit:**
- Active traders who need their assets liquid at all times
- People who prefer full self-custody (hardware wallets)
- High-frequency DeFi users (on-chain protocols may offer better rates)

## My Current Setup

Here's what I actually do on Nexo:

```
Asset allocation on Nexo:
- 40% USDC → Earn at 12% APY (paid in NEXO for +2% bonus)
- 30% ETH → Earn at 7.5% APY (NEXO payout)
- 30% kept as collateral buffer (available for credit line if needed)

Result: meaningful passive yield on stablecoins,
        ETH grows while I hold it long-term
```

I treat the stablecoin yield as a way to offset inflation on my cash reserves.

## How to Sign Up

1. Go to Nexo via my link and create an account
2. Complete KYC (5–30 minutes)
3. Enable 2FA (Google Authenticator or hardware key)
4. Add a withdrawal address to your allowlist before depositing
5. Deposit your first asset and watch daily interest start accruing

Nexo occasionally runs promotions — new users sometimes get bonus NEXO tokens on their first deposit. Check the promo page after signing up.

[Sign up for Nexo →](https://nexo.sjv.io/c/7446760/918878/12544)

## Bottom Line

Nexo is a solid platform for crypto holders who want to put idle assets to work. The earn rates are genuinely competitive — especially on stablecoins. The credit line product is legitimately useful for liquidity without selling.

The risks are real: this is a centralized custodian, not a bank. Use it for assets you're comfortable leaving with a third party, keep your cold storage for long-term holds, and never YOLO your entire portfolio into any single platform.

For what it is — a crypto earn-and-borrow platform — it's one of the better options available in 2026.

[Try Nexo →](https://nexo.sjv.io/c/7446760/918878/12544)
